input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at XAML load time. For runtime theme changes, use **`DynamicResource`**:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by clearing and merging dictionaries:

```csharp
var mergedDictionaries = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDictionaries.Clear();
mergedDictionaries.Add(isDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

## 2. Activity restarts on system dark mode toggle

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when UI mode changes. Prevent this by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigChanges` in **Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs**:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the UI-mode change itself, so it won't restart. You can then detect the system change and swap your `ResourceDictionary` accordingly.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restart on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user changes the system dark mode setting, the Android activity is being recreated. In .NET MAUI, this can be handled by setting `ConfigChanges` on the `Activity` attribute to include `UiMode`.

Let me give a concise answer.
